message:
🌱 Add qdrant: <jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size on ARM64 system mission (#1202)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "qdrant-2474-jemalloc-unsupported-system-page-size-on-arm64-system",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "qdrant: <jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size on ARM64 system",
+    "description": "<jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size on ARM64 system. Community-reported issue.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify qdrant troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your qdrant deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n qdrant -l app.kubernetes.io/name=qdrant\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=qdrant -n qdrant --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review qdrant configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant qdrant configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n qdrant -l app.kubernetes.io/name=qdrant\nkubectl get configmap -n qdrant -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=qdrant\n```\nI am trying to start `qdrant/qdrant:v1.4.1` docker image on a ARM64 machine (a QNAP NAS  with AL324 CPU and 16 Gigabyte RAM). \nFails with memory allocation error.\n\n## Current Behavior\nStart fails with memory allocation errors."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for <jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size on ARM64 system",
+        "description": "Hi , does it fail on the first start?\nNot sure if it is related to cpu architecture, as we test our build on ARM machines, and some part of the cloud is running on ARM.\n\nBut your device looks unusual, maybe you would need to manually disable jemalloc and build from sources\n```yaml\n<jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size\n<jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size\nmemory allocation of 5 bytes failed\n./entrypoint.sh: line 25:     7 Aborted                 ./qdrant $@\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm <jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size on ARM64… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=qdrant -n qdrant --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n qdrant --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Hi , does it fail on the first start?\nNot sure if it is related to cpu architecture, as we test our build on ARM machines, and some part of the cloud is running on ARM.\n\nBut your device looks unusual, maybe you would need to manually disable jemalloc and build from sources",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "<jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size\n<jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size\nmemory allocation of 5 bytes failed\n./entrypoint.sh: line 25:     7 Aborted                 ./qdrant $@",
+        "docker run -it qdrant/qdrant:v1.4.1\n<jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size\n<jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size\nmemory allocation of 5 bytes failed\n./entrypoint.sh: line 25:     7 Aborted                 ./qdrant $@",
+        "docker run -it -m 8G qdrant/qdrant:v1.4.1\n<jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size\n<jemalloc>: Unsupported system page size\nmemory allocation of 5 bytes failed\n./entrypoint.sh: line 25:     7 Aborted                 ./qdrant $@"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "qdrant",
+      "community",
+      "vector-db",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [

+    ],

+      "issue": "https://github.com/qdrant/qdrant/issues/2474",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/qdrant/qdrant"
+    },
+    "reactions": 1,
+    "comments": 43,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },

+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with qdrant installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
